title: pavonia subrotunda
intro: Pavonia subrotunda, also known as the Rose Mallow, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Malvaceae family. It is a flowering plant native to Mexico and Central America, and its leaves and roots have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. Pavonia subrotunda is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues, including wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its leaves and roots can also be used in topical applications to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
